content:
# MATLAB

**Summary**: A high-level programming language and environment used for numerical computation, automation, and machine learning.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2001-sultan|SULTAN, 2001]] used matlab to develop the algorithms for data normalization, Hough transform processing, and neural network training.

- [[2004-ayoub|AYOUB, 2004]] used Matlab to develop the neural network, monitor performance across three datasets, and generate plots.

- [[2009-al-gathe|AL-GATHE, 2009]] used matlab to develop programs for the CAPE (a, m, n) and 3D methods.

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] developed a Mapping Code and an Attenuation Code in MATLAB to process seismic shot gathers.

- [[2009-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2009]] used Matlab software to develop and optimize the Artificial Neural Network models.

- [[2010-mohammed|Mohammed, 2010]] used MATLAB to run the Huthali Method's SQP algorithms.

- [[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011]] used Matlab to develop the code for ANN, FunNet, and SVM models.

- [[2013-kareem|Kareem, 2013]] developed a MATLAB algorithm to solve the coupled system of differential equations for pressure, velocity, and concentration fields.

- [[2013-sibaweihi|Sibaweihi, 2013]] used MATLAB to implement the CMA-ES and DE optimization algorithms.

- [[2013-khamis|Khamis, 2013]] used Matlab to develop the PSO optimization codes.

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] used matlab to solve the 1D, two-phase immiscible simulation scheme using the IMPES concept.

- [[2013-ahmed|AHMED, 2013]] used MATLAB to implement the stochastic optimization algorithms (CMAES, DE, IWO).

- [[2014-ahmed|Ahmed, 2014]] implemented the Differential Evolution algorithm in MATLAB to solve the optimization problem.

- [[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014]] used matlab to calculate the Bouguer gravity anomaly and process synthetic data.

- [[2014-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2014]] used matlab to implement the SVM and T2F algorithms.

- [[2015-adeniji|Adeniji, 2015]] used matlab to implement optimization algorithms like DE, PSO, and SA.

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] used MATLAB to implement the finite difference scheme and solve the flow equations.

- [[2017-al-rubaii|AL-RUBAII, 2017]] used matlab to develop nonlinear regression codes and the PSO algorithm for ROP optimization.

- [[2019-alawi|ALAWI, 2019]] developed a computer model in MATLAB to calculate viscous skin factors and acid volumes.

- [[2021-aldhaif|AlDhaif, 2021]] used MATLAB software to develop all four AI models (ANN, SVM, FN, and ANFIS).

- [[2022-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2022]] used MATLAB for implementing the multi-mesh algorithms and processing results.

- [[2022-aboluhom|Aboluhom, 2022]] developed a 3D wormhole model using MATLAB to implement the mathematical formulations and optimize computation speed.

- [[2022-alkathim|Alkathim, 2022]] used MATLAB to implement the two-scale continuum model and perform statistical calculations.

- [[2022-alnasser|Alnasser, 2022]] used MATLAB to solve the energy balance equation in Laplace space.

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used MATLAB to implement the joint inversion algorithms and parallel computing loops.

- [[2024-alabdrabalnabi|Alabdrabalnabi, 2024]] implemented the numerical algorithms in MATLAB to simulate various injection scenarios.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- All programs for normalization, transformation of input data, and training were implemented in MATLAB. ([[2001-sultan|SULTAN, 2001, p. 137]])

- The software allowed for the monitoring of training, validation, and testing sets simultaneously. ([[2004-ayoub|AYOUB, 2004, p. 66]])

- The Matlab-based programs successfully solved multi-variable equations to determine a, m, and n simultaneously. ([[2009-al-gathe|AL-GATHE, 2009, p. 92]])

- The MATLAB implementation allowed for the automated calculation of semblance maps across multiple velocity iterations. ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009, p. 20]])

- Used to implement the neural network architecture and perform statistical analysis on 4031 data points. ([[2009-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2009, p. 30]])

- The Huthali method uses a MATLAB-based SQP procedure to minimize arrival time residuals. ([[2010-mohammed|Mohammed, 2010, p. 67]])

- Matlab was used to implement various backpropagation functions and kernel selections for AI tools. ([[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011, p. 27]])

- The MATLAB implementation successfully integrated the flow chart logic including iterative updates for porosity and permeability based on mineral volume fraction. ([[2013-kareem|Kareem, 2013, p. 75]])

- CMA-ES and DE were implemented in MATLAB as objective function optimizers to determine optimal well locations and cycle ratios. ([[2013-sibaweihi|Sibaweihi, 2013, p. 14]])

- Three specific codes (main, PSO, and DSE) were developed in Matlab. ([[2013-khamis|Khamis, 2013, p. 105]])

- The numerical solution of the model was found to be stable and consistent with established models in cases without wettibility alteration. ([[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013, p. 20]])

- Used to perform the optimization of parameters including concentration and well placement. ([[2013-ahmed|AHMED, 2013, p. 20]])

- The DE algorithm was coded and coupled with the formulated problem in MATLAB. ([[2014-ahmed|Ahmed, 2014, p. 25]])

- The Matlab function was written to calculate the Bouguer gravity anomaly for the defined models. ([[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014, p. 58]])

- MATLAB was used to calculate RMSE, MAE, and MAPE for both models. ([[2014-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2014, p. 137]])

- Not stated in source ([[2015-adeniji|Adeniji, 2015]])

- The software successfully modeled pressure distributions at various time steps (0.5, 20 days) and wellbore pressures. ([[2016-david|DAVID, 2016, p. 80]])

- Matlab was used to develop three specific codes: a main code, a PSO code, and a DSE function code including the Penalty approach. ([[2017-al-rubaii|AL-RUBAII, 2017, p. 156]])

- The model successfully simulated the diversion effect where the injection rate ratio stabilized at 3.5. ([[2019-alawi|ALAWI, 2019, p. 39]])

- All models were developed using MATLAB's specialized toolboxes for neural networks and fuzzy logic. ([[2021-aldhaif|AlDhaif, 2021, p. 45]])

- Not stated in source (mentioned in techniques list). ([[2022-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2022]])

- The TRF model is equipped with a friendly user interface in MATLAB, allowing for easy control and report exportation. ([[2022-aboluhom|Aboluhom, 2022, p. 54]])

- The 2D model was set in MATLAB with a 300x300 grid block size. ([[2022-alkathim|Alkathim, 2022, p. 59]])

- The energy balance was solved numerically in MATLAB to find temperature at multiple time steps. ([[2022-alnasser|Alnasser, 2022, p. 56]])

- Parallel processing in MATLAB reduced run times by up to 89% for SR data. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 56]])

- MATLAB was used to solve the mathematical formulations for flow, mass transfer, and heat transfer. ([[2024-alabdrabalnabi|Alabdrabalnabi, 2024, p. 93]])
